title: PostgreSQL Deadlock on High-Concurrency Transaction Path
symptoms: PostgreSQL logs showing deadlock detected errors at 150/hour
Order processing service returning intermittent 500 errors
Database lock wait queue growing during peak concurrent transactions
pg_stat_activity showing blocked queries waiting for conflicting locks
Automatic retries consuming additional database capacity
root_cause: Order processing and inventory update services acquiring row-level locks on order_items and inventory tables in opposite order, creating a classic deadlock cycle under high concurrency
resolution: Refactored both services to acquire locks in consistent alphabetical table order (inventory first, then order_items), added advisory locks for critical sections, and implemented optimistic concurrency control for inventory updates
preventive_actions: Establish and enforce consistent lock ordering convention across all services
Implement deadlock detection monitoring with frequency-based alerting
Add advisory locks for multi-table transaction critical sections
Create load testing scenario simulating flash sale concurrency levels
Implement circuit breaker on order processing to handle deadlock storms

timeline.event: Flash sale begins, transaction concurrency spikes
Order error rate exceeds 5%, alerting triggers
Rajesh Kumar investigates, identifies deadlock pattern
Lock ordering conflict between services confirmed
Emergency code fix prepared for consistent lock ordering
Fix deployed, deadlock frequency drops immediately
Deadlock rate back to baseline, order processing stable

embedding_summary: PostgreSQL deadlocks spiking to 150/hour during flash sale due to order processing and inventory services acquiring locks in opposite order. 8% order failure rate, 1,200 failed orders. Resolved by enforcing consistent lock ordering and implementing advisory locks.
